what_input_feature: The 2-byte little-endian e_machine field at input offset 0x12-0x13 must hold 0x003e (62 = EM_X86_64). The switch on e_machine at elf.cc:782 dispatches the true arm only for this exact 16-bit value.
why_winner_satisfies: Winning cmplog seed 2a25258f656af261 carries 3e 00 at offset 0x12-0x13 (little-endian 0x003e = EM_X86_64), and seed 80ff9a7dac09a5e8 carries 3e 00 at offset 0x13-0x14; both supply the exact comparand the switch tests for.
why_loser_doesnt: Naive loser seeds carry 00 at offset 0x12, never the 0x3e byte that the switch arm requires, so they fall through to the default arm and return false.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog intercepts the switch comparison via __sanitizer_cov_trace_cmp callbacks, logs the constant 0x3e operand into the CmpLogObserver buffer, and I2SRandReplace substitutes that exact byte into the input at the e_machine offset; naive has no CMP introspection and must hit 0x003e by blind havoc mutation, which it almost never does.

what_input_feature: Same 2-byte e_machine field at offset 0x12-0x13 must reach 0x003e (EM_X86_64), but value_profile approaches it through progressively closer operand values rather than a single substitution.
why_winner_satisfies: Winning value_profile finds reach 3e 00 at the e_machine offset; the CMP_MAP records each input whose e_machine value lands in a closer Hamming-distance bucket to 0x3e, preserving stepping-stones that eventually match exactly.
why_loser_doesnt: Naive loser seeds hold 00 at offset 0x12 and have no CMP_MAP feedback, so no closer-to-0x3e intermediate is retained and the equality gate stays blocked (resolved 1/10 vs winner 10/10).
mechanism_attribution: value_profile adds CMP_MAP buckets keyed by operand Hamming-distance for the switch comparison; an input whose e_machine value is closer to 0x3e than any seen before is preserved as a new corpus stepping-stone, giving a gradient toward the exact EM_X86_64 constant that naive's edge-only feedback lacks.

what_input_feature: A chunk whose 4-byte type field equals the ASCII tag 'sPLT' (0x73 0x50 0x4c 0x54) at the chunk-type offset (e.g. 0x35-0x38), so png_handle_sPLT allocates info_ptr->splt_palettes and the blocker's `splt_palettes != NULL` test is true.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner seed 3 (cmplog, id=4e93d4e85f61dcd4) carries the bytes 73 50 4c 54 ('sPLT') at offset 0x35; cmplog's CMP buffer logs the libpng chunk-type comparison constant and I2SRandReplace splices the exact 4-byte tag into the input, so splt_palettes becomes non-NULL.
why_loser_doesnt: Every naive loser seed has the chunk-type bytes concentrated on 73 52 47 42 ('sRGB') with no 'sPLT' value at 0x35-0x38, so png never allocates splt_palettes and the branch's NULL pointer stays NULL (loser T=0 at line 565).
mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer for the 4-byte chunk-type comparison and substitutes the matching 'sPLT' literal into the input bytes, manufacturing the exact magic tag that havoc-only naive cannot land by chance.

what_input_feature: The same 4-byte 'sPLT' chunk-type tag, reached via incremental byte progress toward the literal so the chunk-type comparison eventually matches and splt_palettes is allocated.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile keeps a CMP_MAP keyed by operand-distance; partial-prefix matches of the chunk-type bytes toward 'sPLT' are retained as new buckets, letting value_profile climb the Hamming/prefix gradient to the full tag and resolve the branch in 10/10 trials.
why_loser_doesnt: naive has no CMP_MAP feedback; its loser seeds stay pinned on the 'sRGB' tag at 0x35-0x38 with no partial-progress reward toward 'sPLT', so it never assembles the chunk type and remains blocked 10/10.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP distance-bucket feedback rewards inputs whose chunk-type bytes are closer to the 'sPLT' literal, providing a gradient that guides naive's same havoc mutators to the magic tag that plain naive coverage cannot sense.

what_input_feature: A PNG with valid chunk-type and length/CRC fields whose comparisons against libpng's expected literals pass, so deeper chunk-parsing populates info_ptr arrays and png_free_data is later reached with num != -1.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner cmplog seeds carry intact chunk headers (PNG/IHDR/gAMA/sRGB at fixed offsets) and the winner-only coverage reaches per-chunk free paths (tRNS 508, eXIf 621, hIST 641, PLTE 650) that the loser never enters, indicating more chunks parsed to valid state.
why_loser_doesnt: Loser naive seeds corrupt the chunk-type / length / CRC region (e.g. offsets 0x1d-0x1e, 0x2c-0x3d differ with no concentration toward the valid literals), so those chunk comparisons fail and the per-item free branches stay un-hit (loser L=0 on the blocker true side).
mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads intercepted integer/string CMP operands from the CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes the expected chunk-type/CRC literal directly into the matching input offset, letting cmplog clear the chunk-validation comparisons that naive must hit by chance; verified by an I2S-floor dash-row ancestor in the winner lineage.

what_input_feature: A PNG whose chunk-validation comparison operands are driven progressively closer to the expected values, so chunk parsing succeeds and png_free_data is reached with a per-item num != -1.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile resolves the branch in all 10 trials and accumulates a positive hitcount (avg=77) on the blocker, reaching the same per-chunk free paths; CMP_MAP distance buckets reward inputs whose chunk-field comparisons get nearer to matching, steering toward valid chunk structures.
why_loser_doesnt: naive has no CMP_MAP feedback, so its corrupted chunk fields (divergent, unconcentrated bytes at the chunk-type/length offsets) provide no gradient and it stays blocked (loser=0/10, blocked 24.00h).
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP records a Hamming/prefix-distance bucket per CMP; an input whose chunk-field comparison is closer to matching adds a new bucket and is preserved, giving a hill-climbing gradient toward valid chunks that plain edge coverage (naive) lacks.

what_input_feature: The IHDR bit_depth byte (offset 0x18) and color_type byte (offset 0x19) must each pass the prior validity gates (L2622 valid bit_depth set, L2629 valid color_type set) yet form a forbidden pairing: color_type==3 (PALETTE) with bit_depth>8, OR color_type in {2,4,6} with bit_depth<8.
why_winner_satisfies: cmplog discovers seeds whose color_type/bit_depth bytes settle on the specific small-integer comparands the L2636 disjuncts test against; the winner takes the TRUE side at L2636 (T=45 F=0) and resolves on all 10 trials.
why_loser_doesnt: naive only reaches the FALSE side at L2636 (T=0 F=17) and stays blocked on 9/10 trials; its color_type/bit_depth byte combinations never hit a valid-but-forbidden pairing because random byte mutation rarely lands exactly on the enum comparands.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver buffer recording the operands of the color_type==N and bit_depth comparisons at L2636/L2622/L2629, finds the matching input byte at offset 0x18/0x19 and substitutes the other operand, planting the exact enum value needed to enter the forbidden combination.

what_input_feature: Same input feature: a valid-but-disallowed color_type/bit_depth combination at offsets 0x18/0x19 that drives L2636 to its TRUE side.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile resolves on all 10 trials and takes TRUE at L2636; its CMP_MAP distance feedback rewards inputs whose color_type/bit_depth bytes get progressively closer to the disallowed comparand values, building the combination by gradient.
why_loser_doesnt: naive lacks CMP_MAP feedback, so an input that is one byte away from a forbidden combination earns no new coverage and is not retained; naive stays blocked 9/10 trials with L2636 never reaching TRUE.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP records a Hamming/distance bucket for each integer CMP (color_type==N, bit_depth comparisons); an input whose operand pair is closer to matching than any seen before adds a new bucket and is preserved as corpus, letting the fuzzer climb toward the exact forbidden color_type/bit_depth pairing.

what_input_feature: A chunk-type field equal to the 4-byte ASCII FOURCC 'sPLT' (0x73 0x50 0x4c 0x54) at a chunk-name offset, so the equality test chunk_name == png_sPLT takes the true branch.
why_winner_satisfies: cmplog winning seeds reach line 229 with the true side resolved (T=48) and dispatch png_handle_sPLT; the BYTE DIFF shows the winner producing the sPLT FOURCC bytes (0x73 's', 0x50 'P', 0x4c 'L', 0x54 'T') at offsets 0x35/0x1a/0x1b/0x1c that the loser never holds.
why_loser_doesnt: naive seeds keep chunk-name bytes at unrelated values and resolve T=0 at line 229, never matching the exact 4-byte sPLT magic.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog intercepts the chunk_name == png_sPLT integer compare, logs both operands, and I2SRandReplace substitutes the constant operand (png_sPLT FOURCC) into the input bytes that matched the other operand, instantly satisfying the exact 4-byte equality that random havoc cannot brute-force.

what_input_feature: A chunk-type field whose bytes progressively approach the 4-byte sPLT FOURCC, ultimately matching it exactly so chunk_name == png_sPLT is true.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile resolves the true branch in 10/10 trials (avg duration 5.50h vs loser 24.00h), driven by CMP_MAP prefix-distance feedback that preserves each input whose chunk_name operand is closer to png_sPLT than any prior input, climbing the gradient to the exact match.
why_loser_doesnt: naive lacks the CMP_MAP distance feedback, gets no signal for partial-match progress on the chunk_name compare, and stays blocked (resolved=0/10, blocked=10).
mechanism_attribution: value_profile writes the chunk_name == png_sPLT operand pair into a CMP_MAP keyed by Hamming/prefix-distance bucket; an input whose chunk_name is closer to the sPLT FOURCC than before adds a new bucket and is retained as corpus, giving a hill-climbing gradient toward the exact 4-byte constant that naive's edge-only feedback cannot follow.

what_input_feature: The IHDR color_type byte at input offset 0x19 (buf[9], the 10th byte of the 13-byte IHDR payload) must hold value 0x03 = PNG_COLOR_TYPE_PALETTE; the switch on png_ptr->color_type then takes the line 883 case body.
why_winner_satisfies: Every cmplog winning seed carries 0x03 at offset 0x19, and the BYTE DIFF column shows the winner set is concentrated entirely on 03 there (03(.)x30), while DIVERGENT BRANCHES shows line 883 firing W T=30 F=0.
why_loser_doesnt: naive loser seeds hold 00/04/06 at offset 0x19 (GRAY_with_other, RGB, RGBA paths), never the exact 0x03 PALETTE value, so line 883's true side is never taken (L T=0 F=10).
mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver buffer recording the switch comparison color_type vs the case constant 3, finds the loser's color_type byte in the input, and substitutes the constant 3 at offset 0x19 — directly solving the equality the loser cannot reach by blind havoc.

what_input_feature: The same single byte at offset 0x19 (color_type) must reach 0x03; the switch comparison color_type==PNG_COLOR_TYPE_PALETTE is the gate.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile resolves the branch in 10/10 trials and the winner-resolving byte set is concentrated on 03 at offset 0x19, satisfying the case-3 comparison.
why_loser_doesnt: naive holds non-3 color_type values (00/04/06) and blocks 9/10 trials; without comparison feedback it cannot home in on the exact constant 3.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP records the operand-distance bucket between color_type and the case constant 3; an input whose color_type byte is Hamming-closer to 3 than any prior input is preserved as corpus, giving a gradient toward 0x03 that naive's bare edge feedback lacks.

what_input_feature: The IHDR color_type byte at file offset 0x19 must hold the value 0x03 (PNG_COLOR_TYPE_PALETTE) so that the equality test info_ptr->color_type == PNG_COLOR_TYPE_PALETTE is true.
why_winner_satisfies: Every winner-resolving seed carries 0x03 at offset 0x19 (W bytes 03(.)x30, all 30 occurrences), exactly matching the PALETTE constant compared at line 276.
why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds never hold 0x03 at offset 0x19 (L bytes 00(.)x7 04(.)x2 06(.)x1) so the color_type equality test is always false.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog intercepts the integer CMP at line 276 via __sanitizer_cov_trace_cmp1, logging the constant operand 3; I2SRandReplace then substitutes 3 into the color_type byte, deterministically solving the equality where naive must blind-mutate the exact value.

what_input_feature: The IHDR color_type byte at offset 0x19 must equal 0x03; value_profile rewards inputs whose color_type byte moves closer (smaller Hamming distance) to the compared constant 3.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner seeds reach 0x03 at offset 0x19 (W bytes 03(.)x30); value_profile preserves intermediate inputs that narrowed the CMP-operand distance to the constant, climbing the gradient to an exact match.
why_loser_doesnt: Loser seeds stall at non-3 color_type values (L bytes 00(.)x7 04(.)x2 06(.)x1); naive lacks the CMP_MAP distance feedback so partial progress toward 3 is not preserved as corpus.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile writes each CMP into a CMP_MAP keyed by (PC, operand-distance bucket); an input whose color_type byte is closer to the constant 3 opens a new bucket and is retained, building a Hamming-distance gradient toward equality that naive cannot follow.

what_input_feature: An input where the byte at the parse cursor (after optional 4-byte encoding detection) is 0x00, satisfying the integer comparison CUR == 0 at parser.c:10852.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds carry NUL bytes early in the buffer (e.g. seed 1 has 5c 0a 00 00 00 around offset 0x04-0x07 and seed 2 has 0a 00 80 at offset 0x05-0x07); the cmplog winner resolved the branch in 8/10 trials, taking the W-marked true-branch at lines 10853-10855.
why_loser_doesnt: naive loser seeds begin with printable prolog/URL content (e.g. 127772.xml, <?xml vdrsion, http://www.w3) with no 0x00 at the cursor, so CUR is nonzero and they always take the false branch (T=0 F=10 at L10852); naive blocked 10/10.
mechanism_attribution: CUR == 0 compiles to an integer CMP against the literal 0; cmplog's CMP interception logs the (current_byte, 0) operand pair and I2SRandReplace scans the input for the current byte and substitutes the other operand (0x00) at that offset, directly synthesizing the NUL at the cursor that naive's blind havoc must stumble onto.

what_input_feature: An input where the byte at the parse cursor is 0x00; value_profile additionally rewards intermediate inputs whose cursor byte is Hamming-close to 0 (small-magnitude bytes), giving a distance gradient toward CUR == 0.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile winner seeds reach the NUL cursor (resolved 9/10, true branch T=3 F=0 at L10852 in the W role); seed bytes show low-value/NUL runs (seed 1: 00 00 00 at 0x0a region; seed 2: 00 at 0x07) that the CMP_MAP distance bucket can climb toward incrementally.
why_loser_doesnt: naive lacks the CMP_MAP distance feedback, so inputs whose cursor byte is merely near 0 (small bytes) are not preserved as corpus; naive blocked 10/10 with avg duration 24.00h and never hit the branch (avg hitcount on branch loser=0).
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP records the operand-distance bucket for the CUR == 0 comparison; an input producing a cursor byte closer to 0 than any prior input adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is retained, building a Hamming-distance gradient that drives the cursor byte down to exactly 0x00 — feedback naive does not have.

what_input_feature: A SELECT statement whose expression carries many ASCII integer literals joined by arithmetic operators (e.g. 0x35 '5', 0x2b '+', 0x2f '/', 0x2d '-'), driving codeInteger through the true branch at 102982:9.
why_winner_satisfies: The cmplog winners are 108-171 byte SELECT bodies densely packed with digit '5' (0x35), '+' (0x2b), '/' (0x2f) and '-' (0x2d) bytes from offset 0x07 onward, exercising codeInteger 32 times on the true side (W L102982 T=32).
why_loser_doesnt: The naive losers are short SELECT 1; / garbage inputs (9-49 bytes) with no operator-joined literal run; they never take the true branch (L L102982 T=0 F=32).
mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes one CMP operand for the other at matching offsets, planting the integer-token byte patterns the parser/codegen compares against; the winner seed's ancestor chain contains I2S-floor dash rows.

what_input_feature: Same structural feature (operator-joined integer-literal SELECT expression) reaching codeInteger's true branch, approached incrementally by closing CMP-operand distance.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile resolved 10/10 with avg hitcount 112 on the branch, far above the loser's 1, indicating it repeatedly reaches and takes the codeInteger codegen path.
why_loser_doesnt: naive stays blocked 8/10 at the same branch with hitcount 1, never accumulating the literal-arithmetic structure needed.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP records a Hamming/prefix-distance bucket per CMP; an input that brings an integer-literal comparison closer to matching adds a new bucket and is preserved, providing a gradient toward the byte values the codegen branch requires.
